# BIM Command Center for Revit - Store Listing Draft

## Product Name

BIM Command Center for Revit

## Short Description

A Revit productivity dashboard for BIM coordinators and MEP teams, combining model health checks, workset review, auto-save support, MEP splitting tools and clash coordination utilities in one command center.

## Long Description

BIM Command Center for Revit brings frequently used BIM coordination tools into one unified Revit add-in. It is designed for BIM managers, coordinators and MEP modelers who need faster access to model review, workset control, routine automation and clash follow-up workflows.

The add-in provides a dockable dashboard and ribbon commands that reduce switching between separate tools. Teams can review model status, manage worksets, run MEP productivity commands, support auto-save workflows and prepare clash-related views from a single Revit environment.

Recommended first release scope:

- Unified BIM dashboard and ribbon launcher
- Model Health Dashboard
- Workset Dashboard
- Revit Auto Save / Auto Sync support
- MEP Splitter commands
- Clash point and coordination view utilities
- Unit conversion tools, if validated for release
- Local To Do List, if validated for release

## Target Users

- BIM managers
- VDC coordinators
- Revit MEP modelers
- Design coordination teams
- Contractors and engineering firms using Revit for production coordination

## Key Benefits

- One dashboard for multiple Revit productivity tools
- Faster access to repeated coordination commands
- Model health and workset checks for BIM management
- MEP-focused automation for daily production work
- Coordination utilities that support clash review and follow-up
- Designed around practical Revit project workflows

## System Requirements

- Autodesk Revit 2024, 2025 and 2026, after validation
- Windows 10 or Windows 11
- .NET Framework 4.8 and/or .NET 8 Desktop Runtime depending on selected Revit version
- Microsoft Edge WebView2 Runtime if the dashboard web panel is enabled

## Important Disclosures To Finalize

- Confirm whether the product sends data to any local server, cloud service, AI service or external API.
- Confirm whether name, phone, email, machine ID or license information is collected.
- Confirm whether model information leaves the local computer.
- Confirm whether internet access is required for licensing, AI functions or update checks.

- Revit ribbon with BIM Command Center button
- Main dockable dashboard
- Model Health Dashboard result
- Workset Dashboard result
- MEP Splitter command workflow
- Clash point or section box workflow
- Settings/license screen without private information

Detailed shot list: `SCREENSHOT_VIDEO_SHOTLIST.md`.

## Demo Video Script

1. Open Revit and show the BIM Command Center ribbon.
2. Launch the dockable dashboard.
3. Run a model health or workset check.
4. Execute one MEP productivity command.
5. Show a clash coordination utility.
6. Close with settings/support location.

Detailed script: `SCREENSHOT_VIDEO_SHOTLIST.md`.

## Pricing Recommendation

Recommended launch model:

- Subscription app with 30-day free trial.
- Individual: USD 19/month or USD 190/year.
- Team 5-Pack: USD 79/month or USD 790/year, if Store/payment workflow supports it.
- Enterprise: custom pricing for deployment, onboarding and support.
- One license per named user or machine, depending on the final entitlement implementation.
- Offer a support email for activation and onboarding issues.

Do not launch external AI features as the first paid scope unless operating cost, privacy and customer data handling are fully controlled.

## Excluded From First Commercial Release

- `RevitAddin.MepBim`
- MEP·BIM Coordination Console browser dashboard
- Any entitlement, installer payload or Store listing claim tied to `RevitAddin.MepBim`
